what: Koroks are tiny hidden forest spirits scattered across Hyrule (Surface, Sky, and Depths); each one you uncover by solving a small environmental puzzle hands you a Korok Seed, which you trade to Hestu to permanently expand your weapon, bow, and shield pouches so you can carry more gear.
hestu: You first meet Hestu on the Surface just northeast of New Serenne Stable (between the stable and the Lindor's Brow Skyview Tower in West Necluda) — talking to him starts the "Hestu's Concerns" quest. After you clear your FIRST temple he relocates to Lookout Landing (he stands near the general store / central plaza, with no upgrade limit there), and once you reach the Korok Forest deep in the Lost Woods he settles there permanently.

puzzleTypes.see: A single small rock sitting by itself somewhere odd — on a stump, a ledge, a rooftop, the tip of a pillar, or surrounded by water.
puzzleTypes.do: Walk up and lift the rock (Ultrahand, or just grab and throw it) to reveal the Korok hiding underneath.

puzzleTypes.see: A suspicious pile of leaves or a lump of ice, often glinting or out of place.
puzzleTypes.do: Burn the leaf pile (a fire arrow or any flame) or melt the ice, then lift the rock that was underneath.

puzzleTypes.see: A circle, line, or swirl of stones with one stone obviously missing — a clear gap in the shape.
puzzleTypes.do: Find the matching loose rock nearby and use Ultrahand to set it into the empty spot, completing the pattern.

puzzleTypes.see: A small tree-like tripod or stone holder with a hollow center, with a loose boulder sitting close by.
puzzleTypes.do: Use Ultrahand to lift the matching boulder and nestle it snugly into the cradle so it sits centered.

puzzleTypes.see: Two rock or statue formations that are almost identical, but one is missing a piece the other has.
puzzleTypes.do: Use Ultrahand to move a stone/piece so both arrangements match each other exactly.

puzzleTypes.see: A single faintly glowing yellow flower, usually in a high or scenic spot, that vanishes when you get near.
puzzleTypes.do: Touch it, then chase it as it reappears a little further on, in sequence; keep following until the flower turns white, and the Korok pops out when you reach that last one.

puzzleTypes.see: A large glowing dandelion / white flower puff standing on its own.
puzzleTypes.do: Slash the dandelion so the fluff launches into the air, then catch/touch the floating seed before it lands.

puzzleTypes.see: A tree stump with a leaf pattern carved on top; stepping on it spawns a glowing target ring a short distance away with a countdown.
puzzleTypes.do: Stand on the stump to start it, then sprint, glide, or use Zonai devices to reach the target ring before the timer runs out. Speed-up food helps.

puzzleTypes.see: You step on a leaf stump and a target appears above a SECOND stump nearby — no timer, but touching the ground resets it.
puzzleTypes.do: Get from the first stump to the second without ever touching the ground — paraglide, climb, or build a path with Ultrahand/Zonai.

puzzleTypes.see: A leaf-marked tree stump set into a ceiling, cliff overhang, or the underside of a platform, just out of reach above you.
puzzleTypes.do: Stand directly beneath it and use Ascend to rise up through the stump.

puzzleTypes.see: A stump on a Sky Island or high ledge that, when you stand on it, shoots a glowing orb out into open air.
puzzleTypes.do: Step on the stump, then immediately leap off and dive to catch the orb in midair before it falls.

puzzleTypes.see: A ring of lily pads or flowers arranged in a circle on a pond, with a high point above it.
puzzleTypes.do: Climb to the high spot and dive straight down into the center of the ring of lilies.

puzzleTypes.see: An acorn-shaped jar or hollow log hanging from a branch or structure, with an acorn visible inside.
puzzleTypes.do: Shoot the acorn with an arrow from your bow.

puzzleTypes.see: You step on a stump and wooden-and-leaf balloons rise into the air around you.
puzzleTypes.do: Stay on the platform and pop every balloon with arrows (bullet-time mid-air shots help) — don't step off.

puzzleTypes.see: A pinwheel windmill with several glowing target rings appearing around it when you approach.
puzzleTypes.do: Stand near the pinwheel and shoot down all the targets with arrows before they vanish, without straying too far.

puzzleTypes.see: A lone Korok wearing a big backpack with a '!' over its head, looking stuck — its friend is somewhere in the distance (often marked by a green smoke signal).
puzzleTypes.do: Get the backpack Korok to its friend any way you can — carry it, or build a cart / raft / vehicle with Ultrahand and Zonai parts and haul it there. Reuniting them pays 2 seeds.

puzzleTypes.see: A broken structure with an obvious gap — a roof with a missing piece, a cart needing a wheel, or loose blocks beside an incomplete shape.
puzzleTypes.do: Use Ultrahand to grab the nearby loose piece and slot it into the gap so the structure is whole again.

puzzleTypes.see: A wooden cork or plug stuck into a log, pond, or hole, usually chained or wedged in tight.
puzzleTypes.do: Use Ultrahand to yank the cork out, or fling a heavy boulder/rocket at it to pop it free.

puzzleTypes.see: A small statue, altar, or bowl with an empty offering plate (a carving sometimes hints what it wants).
puzzleTypes.do: Drop the right fruit into the bowl — usually an apple, but some want a specific item like a Mighty Banana.

hotspots: Great Sky Island (the tutorial sky area, ~17–19 Koroks) — gentle intro puzzles: lift-the-rock, an Ultrahand block-fit, a dive-for-the-orb stump, and balloon shots that teach the patterns before you reach the Surface.

Lookout Landing and the surrounding Hyrule Field — once you land, the open field around the central hub is dotted with lift-the-rock, complete-the-circle, and flower-trail Koroks within easy walking distance.
New Serenne Stable / Lindor's Brow area (West Necluda) — right where you first meet Hestu, so you can trade as you go; nearby stumps and rocks make it a natural early farming spot.
Any Stable — stables almost always have a Korok at or near them (commonly a hidden rock or a 'carry me to my friend' buddy), so checking each stable you pass is reliable.

Roadsides and bridges everywhere — Koroks love road edges, the ends of bridges, and lone landmarks (a single tree, a rock arch); scanning these as you travel turns up flower trails, rock circles, and carry buddies constantly.
notes: Sources agree there are 1,000 Korok Seeds total in the base game but only ~800 puzzle LOCATIONS — the difference is the carry-the-buddy reunions, which each pay 2 seeds. You only need 421 seeds to fully max every slot via Hestu (153 weapon, 160 shield, 108 bow), so the remaining ~579 are purely completionist (the reward for all 1,000 is Hestu's Gift, a famous gag trophy). Verified-but-omitted: a few guides also list a 'follow the sparkles/wisps' chase and a 'red signpost arrows converge' puzzle; both are real in TotK but were left out here to keep the 19 types clean (the sparkle-chase plays like the flower-trail; the signposts point to a hidden Korok). Puzzle counts vary by guide (~9 'main' families up to ~19 when every variant is split); the 19 here are each independently confirmed. Several types — Ultrahand build/repair, cork-pull, rock-in-ring via Ultrahand, the carry-buddy vehicle build, ceiling-stump Ascend — are TotK-signature and were not in BotW. Solutions are open-ended in TotK, so the 'do' steps describe the intended/easiest route, not the only one.
